top_insights: If you don't design your routine, life will assign you one.
Storytelling isn't fluff; it's the highest-leverage asset you own.
Real wealth comes from solving problems, not chasing viral trends.
Discipline isn't hard; you're just addicted to cheap distractions.
Stop waiting for permission. Build the system, then sell the result.
hook_patterns: Specific data points or historical facts presented as insights
Direct, punchy declarations of personal philosophy or rules
angles: Deconstructing popular myths with hard data or historical context
Sharing specific, actionable blueprints for financial or personal growth
Highlighting the contrast between 'assigned' routines and self-created systems
Showcasing real-world examples of creators solving problems independently
Reframing success as a result of discipline rather than luck or hacks
emotional_drivers: Validation of personal beliefs or status
Ambition and the desire for success
emerging_narrative: A shift towards practical, high-leverage actions where individuals take control of their financial and personal destiny by rejecting overhyped trends, building their own systems, and leveraging storytelling to create tangible value.

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: “Cha-ching” $5,000 hit my PayPal account. “This has to be a typo...” I thought to myself. So I messaged him. “Hey, did you mean to send $5,000?” His response: “Yeah, is that enough?” And just like that, Ghostwriting completely changed my life trajectory. Here’s the story

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: Steve Jobs once said: "The most powerful person is the storyteller." And luckily, storytelling is a skill. But most people have never tried to learn – because no one showed them where to start. So here are 6 of the best storytelling threads (so you can start learning today):

viral_examples.author: dickiebush
viral_examples.text: The single most powerful habit for personal growth: Journaling. Over the past 5 years, I've journaled every single morning—and along the way, I've: •Answered over 1,000 questions •Tested every app, pen, & notebook But I always return to a pen, paper, and these 5 prompts:

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: Every picture of JP Morgan published in his lifetime (and thus nearly every picture published since) was heavily retouched. This is what he actually looked like.

viral_examples.author: paulg
viral_examples.text: 65 doctors, nurses, and paramedics told the New York Times what they saw in Gaza. What they saw was a pattern of children being shot in the head.

winning_patterns: Specific data points or historical facts presented as insights
Direct, punchy declarations of personal philosophy or rules
